mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS rises, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, creating a negative feedback loop that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces near-term corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of declining INDPRO months, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly growth in real gross domestic product reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate profit margins, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0% triggers a liquidity contraction that compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of observations above the threshold, or the predictive slope flattens to zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts signals expanding residential investment and construction activity that multiplies through supply chains, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of rising HOUST periods, or the signal fails during housing market corrections characterized by inventory gluts.
